reviews.review_description: This game is definitely not the most polished game you will find on App Lab. But it is simple, and a lot of fun for younger kids who love to cruise around the different underwater environments collecting different sea creatures. I wouldn't necessarily consider it an educational game, since there are a few issues in size and animal selection. (A hippo walking around the ocean floor for example) I also wish there was a better way to examine the animal models without them disappearing when you get to close and "collect" them.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback! I will look at adding an animal viewing area with animals at real life scale that can be simply viewed into ZOSU Ocean.  Also I will take a look at some of the more exaggerated animal scales (scaled-up for game effect and to maintain high frame per second performance) and selections as well [the hippo :) ] I have another game “ZOSU Zoo” with many land animals and all the sea creatures can be simply viewed as well.

reviews.review_description: As a game it is a little limited at the moment but it's actually just a wonderful way to relax swimming around finding an impressive amount of undersea life that you can get close to and collect. I honestly found myself just floating around in this cool underwater world more than trying to get any high score. Great way to relax :)

reviews.review_title: A Nice Attempt That Needs Some Work
reviews.review_description:
NOTE: Received product for free.
This is a 'capture as many sea creatures as you can before the timer runs out' game, and nothing more. It is a pleasant enough game, but is lacking in various areas: (1) There is little to no sense of feeling you are underwater, and instead it feels like you are flying through the air in semi-jerky movements , (2) the sea creatures are swimming in place, so there is no joy in trying to follow them around, and swim alongside these majestic creatures, (3) the game is so intent on being time-based that you are running around and can't take the time to slow down and take in the atmosphere, (4) the environments look huge but somehow have a claustrophobic feel to them, (5) there are some potentially scary-looking fish in the sea, but just swimming in place makes them a harmless distraction that is easy to ignore.

There is appeal to this game, and I can see the potential in it, but as is, I fear you may discover that despite the available number of levels, the basic gameplay doesn't change in a way I can detect, and after a few levels I felt like I had seen it all.

If this is your first time playing an underwater game, you will likely enjoy more than I, but having played a number of underwater games since the Oculus Rift days, this game will need further work to increase my enjoyment.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the detailed feedback I am looking at it for my next update! Also most fish are actually swimming in big patterns,  but yes I will take a look at making an improvement with some of the idling fish in smaller levels and everything else mentioned!

reviews.review_description: Kudos to the developer for this one - it made me smile from ear-to-ear.   It's very much like a colorful dreamscape that I lost myself in for quite a few minutes.  The fish don't move (they are animated but remain stationary) but that didn't detract from the overall experience.  I did genuinely laugh out loud when I saw a hippo and the developers face on a fish!   I wish you every success with your future projects - thank you for this, it was fun!

This game will surprise you. Game is probably not the right word. This is a trippy, surreal, baffling jaunt in an ocean of incongruity. Nothing is clear. Hold a trigger to advance, collide with all manner of creatures, and just smile. I could play this all day. It's genuinely an odd, off-kilter experience that I highly recommend.

reviews.review_title: Not so much a game, more an experience
reviews.review_description: It's nice seeing ocean life everywhere, with a big variety of animals such as tuna fish, octopuses, and a ton more I don't remember the names of. plus a lot roaming around the world / level. Seeing turtles the size of a car or an octopus making you feel small, its pretty cool!
Heaps of potential, a fun relaxing experience, visually very good. Interface could do with some work but i am sure that will come.
This experience, while in need of some fine tuning here and there, is interesting. I like the selection of ocean life moving around among the plants and creatures is fun.
After initially coming off a little odd the title coalesced and made a lot more sense as I went on. I highly recommend it.

Cool little experience but not much of a game. Adding more mechanics in a challenge mode could be fun like time limits, fuel limits, leaderboard for it. But it was fun to "fly around" and see the fish.

ZOSU Ocean is a very relaxing game in Virtual Reality. Zosu Ocean's Gameplay lets you collect species of animals in a time limit to help them from going extinct. Enjoy beautiful scenery and relaxing sounds!
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Jl2lFxymD20
